America’s energy problem isn’t a lack of resources – it’s a failure of political will to build the infrastructure to move them. Permitting reform costs taxpayers zero dollars but will unlock massive economic growth & lower utility bills. 

It’s time to get America building again! https://t.co/iBz77QKEt4

I could have stayed on the sidelines and complained about our broken permitting system, but I believe that if you see a problem this serious, you ought to do what you can to help fix it.

It’s time to get America building again. 🏗️ https://t.co/FGodm4LFmg

In my few short months in the Senate, I have known Lindsey to be an honorable and principled man. He represented his home state with charm and class, and is leaving behind an incredible legacy of service. 

Our prayers are with his loved ones and all who mourn his loss.

It's an honor to introduce my first bill, the American Energy and Mineral Infrastructure Act, with strong stakeholder support from across industries. 

This isn’t a bill just for one interest group – it’s going to help EVERYONE build again.

In order to live up to the great American legacy as a nation of builders and innovators, we MUST reform our permitting process.

I’m grateful to have the support of @PacificLegal and so many industry leaders on the American Energy and Mineral Infrastructure Act. Thank you to Josh

Nationwide, demand on our grid is anticipated to grow astronomically in the coming years, but a broken permitting system means we cannot build fast enough to keep up with that demand. If nothing changes, blackouts will become commonplace - and not just in blue cities like NYC.

When Americans see their energy bills rise, they look for answers. The truth is, our broken permitting system is choking supply and driving up costs. 

I joined @BloombergTV to discuss my new bill to fix this system, increase competition, and deliver relief to consumers. https://t.co/GW8bxYAjjf

The surge in AI data centers is putting an unprecedented strain on our electric grid, but a broken federal permitting system is crushing our manufacturers, delaying urgent projects, and driving up electricity bills for everyone else.

We need to build, and we need to build now.

From bipartisan, bicameral support for real reform to a unified voice from our cross-sector industry panel, there is true excitement behind the movement to GET AMERICA BUILDING AGAIN!

Thank you to my colleagues @SenLummis, @SenRickScott, @SenKatieBritt & @SenatorLankford for joining me as original co-sponsors. 

The time is now for meaningful permitting reform!

I’ve spent the first few months of my tenure in the Senate listening to stakeholders, talking with my colleagues, and engaging with everyone I come into contact with on the urgent need to pass permitting reform.

Today, I’m putting all those conversations and ideas on paper with

NOW is the time, and we can’t get it done soon enough.

Let’s GET AMERICA BUILDING AGAIN!

A very special thank you to my governor, @GovStitt, who had the vision to use the senate vacancy for an all-hands-on-deck push to pass something real to help America build again.

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 10 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Alan Armstrong. The social-media posture reads as constructive or diplomatic signal because the strongest matched frame is support, partnership, or official-contact language. Top matched terms: Oil / Energy.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around support, partnership, or official-contact language. I count 0 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 2 security-frame hits, 3 supportive or diplomatic hits, 0 humanitarian/minority hits, and 10 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2026-07-16T18:17:17Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.
